titlestmt.titleproper: Gail and Stephen Rudin autograph collection6875
titlestmt.author: Compiled by RMC Staff

publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2022-09-08 18:52:35 UTC.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.unittitle: Gail and Stephen Rudin autograph collection
did.abstract: 251 letters and other documents recording the achievements of notable artists, politicians, scientists, architects, businessmen and women, journalists, musicians, and educators.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524rareref@cornell.eduhttp://rmc.library.cornell.edu

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: 251 letters and other documents recording the achievements of notable artists, politicians, scientists, architects, businessmen and women, journalists, musicians, and educators.
Additional letters (ca. 107).

list.head: Signed Books
list.item: Little America: Aerial Exploration in the Antarctic, the flight to the South Pole, by Richard Byrd.
My Life's History, by Grandma Moses.
The Deposition of Alexander G. Bell In The Suit Brought By The United States To Annul the Bell Patents, signed and addressed to Charles Swan.

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Gail and Stephen Rudin autograph collection, #6875. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

did.unittitle: Anthony, Susan B. Addressee: Mrs. Watson Writer's location: Rochester, New York

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "To get the right to vote secured to the women of this nation!" First Day Covers and Coins are also included in this file. Rudin # A-1

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "What America Means To Me". Rudin # A-2

did.unittitle: Audubon, John J. Addressee: John Kirk Townsend Writer's location: Charleston, South Carolina

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Refers to a possible new bird species and requests research be done as to whether the species is a distinct one or not. Is writing the second volume of the Ornithological Biography. With this lot is the program for the first day of issue ceremony of the 1985 Audubon stamp. Rudin # A-3

did.unittitle: Powell, Robert Baden Addressee: C.V. Swan Writer's location: London

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Congratulating Swan and his group of Scouts on their work with the Coastwatching Service during the War. Rudin # A-4

did.unittitle: Barnum, P.T. Addressee: Robert Collyer Writer's location: Bridgeport, Connecticut

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Discusses his bout of exhaustion and his resolve to "take it easy". Rudin # A-5

did.unittitle: Barnum, P.T. Addressee: Mr. H.P.R. Holt Writer's location: Bridgeport, Connecticut

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Offers sage advice for happiness and salvation. Rudin # A-6

did.unittitle: Bartholdi, Frederic Auguste Addressee: Cheri Madame Writer's location: New York

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The sculptor attempts to raise funds to build the Statue of Liberty. In French, with translation. Rudin # A-7

did.unittitle: Bartholdi, Frederic Auguste Writer's location: Paris

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Pamphlet entitled "La Statue de la Liberte, Eclairant Le Monde" signed and inscribed by Bartholdi. (36 pages plus two fold-out plates). Rudin # A-8

did.unittitle: Bartholdi, Frederic Auguste Writer's location: New York

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1886 silk souvenir ribbon printed around the time of the dedication of the Statue of Liberty. Rudin # A-8A

did.unittitle: Barton, Clara Addressee: Lucy Ellen Guernsey Writer's location: Johnstown, Pennsylvania

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: In the aftermath of the devastating Johnston Flood, Barton gushes "It takes great courage to begin again the struggle for existence". Rudin # A-9

did.unittitle: Barton, Clara Addressee: Charles S. Smith Writer's location: Washington, DC

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Barton requests assistance for food in the first overseas mission of the Red Cross for The Russian Famine of 1892. Rudin # A-10

did.unittitle: Barton, Clara Addressee: Col. S.W. Hill Writer's location: Constantinople

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Barton describes relief efforts in Turkey after the Armenian massacres. Rudin # A-11

did.unittitle: Barton, Clara Addressee: Mrs. Donald McLean Writer's location: Glen Echo, MD

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: An embattled Barton resists attacks on her conduct as Red Cross President. Rudin # A-12

did.unittitle: Beauregard, G.T. - Addressee: Confederate Commissioners Writer's location: Charleston, South Carolina

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "Sumter not evacuated, Anderson is still working on its defenses". See also letter Claim for Impressed Slave who died while working on Ft. Sumter (S. Carolina), Rudin #151 in collection #4681. Rudin # A-13

did.unittitle: Beecher, Henry Ward Addressee: "My dear Sir" Writer's location: n.p.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Beecher agrees to marry couple under unusual circumstances - a man wants to marry his deceased wife's sister. Rudin # A-14

did.unittitle: Bell, Alexander Graham Addressee: "My dear Senator" (Unnamed) Writer's location: Washington, D.C.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Bell urges Census amendment for the hearing impaired. Rudin # A-15

did.unittitle: Bell, Alexander Graham Addressee: Charles H. Swan Writer's location: Boston

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "The Deposition of Alexander G. Bell In The Suit Brought By The United States To Annul the Bell Patents". Book inscribed to Charles H. Swan, Bell's Patent Attorney who also wrote the introduction to the book. See also Selden, Edison, Tesla and Wright for other patents. See separate material below. Rudin # A-16

did.unittitle: Bell, Alexander Graham Writer's location: United States Patent Office

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Patent for Hydrodome, hydro-aeroplane and the like. Rudin # A-17

did.unittitle: Ben Gurion, David Addressee: Zelman Aran Minister of Education Writer's location: n.p.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Discusses the importance of educating the poor in Israel. (In Hebrew, with translation.) Included is a signed title page from his book, "Memoirs", AmOved Publishers, Tel Aviv 1971. Rudin # A-18

did.unittitle: Berlin, Irving Addressee: Mrs. Dick Bernie Writer's location: New York

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Berlin writes about his army tour playing for the troops. Rudin # A-19

did.unittitle: Bernstein, Leonard Writer's location: n.p. (Boston)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Early essay by Bernstein - "I am filled all the more with the desire for a musical life". Rudin # A-20

did.unittitle: Bernstein, Leonard Addressee: Renee Longy-Miquelle Writer's location: New York, NY

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Bernstein writes to his former teacher and extemporizes on his ambitions for transforming American music. Rudin # A-21

did.unittitle: Bernstein, Leonard Addressee: Hans Heinsheimer Writer's location: Cuernavaca, Mexico

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Bernstein expresses his concerns over Trouble in Tahiti, the Age of Anxiety and his Anniversaries for Piano. Rudin # A-22

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Documents, letters and patents pertaining to the Bobbie Skate, a forerunner of the Roller Blade. Rudin # M-82

did.unittitle: Borden, Lizzie Writer's location: Fall River, MA

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: An album containing a large collection of manuscripts, documents and newspaper clippings all pertaining to the murder of Andrew and Abby Borden and the arrest and trial of Lizzie Borden for the murders. Rudin # A-22A

did.unittitle: Bridgeman, Laura Addressee: Mr. Brown Writer's location: n.p.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: One of the first deaf and blind people to be educated. Declines an invitation. Rudin # A-23

did.unittitle: Bryan, William Jennings Addressee: Paul Fuller, Jr. Writer's location: En Route to San Francisco

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter written shortly after he resigned as Secretary of State. Rudin # A-24

did.unittitle: Bunche, Ralph Johnson Addressee: Miss. Doris Busby Writer's location: New York

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Bunche defines the "basic tenets of the American way of life" for a high school history class. Rudin # A-25

did.unittitle: Burbank, Luther Addressee: Katharine L. Ward Writer's location: Santa Rosa, California

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Thanks his correspondent for flower and herbal books and comments on his new puppy, "more intelligent in many ways than the average citizen". Rudin # A-26

did.unittitle: Burger, Warren Addressee: Walter Bitterling Writer's location: Washington, D.C.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Burger compares John Kennedy to Lyndon Johnson. Rudin # A-26A

did.unittitle: Burr, Aaron Addressee: A.B. (Self) Writer's location: New York

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Aaron Burr draws check from Manhattan Company for $500 made out to himself. Rudin # A-27

did.unittitle: Butler, Nicholas Murray Addressee: Reverand Paul R. Hickok Writer's location: New York City

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Butler continues his crusade against the recent implementation of prohibition. Rudin # A-28

did.unittitle: Byrd, Richard Addressee: To his wife, Marie Writer's location: Byrd Arctic Expedition S.S. Chantier

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A series of four autograph letters written to his wife shortly before take-off for the North Pole. Rudin # A-29

did.unittitle: Byrd, Richard Writer's location: New York

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Byrd's flight to the North Pole and the Arctic flight of the dirigible "Norge" are covered in seven New York Times issues. Rudin # A-30

did.unittitle: Byrd, Richard Writer's location: G.P. Putnam's Sons New York

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "Little America: Aerial Exploration in the Antarctic, The Flight to the South Pole". See separated material below. Rudin # A-31

did.unittitle: Byrd, Richard Addressee: Mrs. E.R. Newell Writer's location: Boston, MA

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Byrd asks a supporter for funds to purchase Eskimo dogs for the 1933-34 Antarctic expedition. Rudin # A-32

did.unittitle: Byrd, Richard Writer's location: G.P. Putnam's Sons New York

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The book "Alone" describes Byrd's Antarctic expedition. See separated material below. Rudin # A-33

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Stamps and First Day Covers - commemorating Byrd's flights over the South Pole. Rudin # A-34

did.unittitle: Carnegie, Andrew Addressee: Mrs. Grover Cleveland Writer's location: New York

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Comments on the progress of Cleveland's health and suggests that doctors order him Scotch Whiskey which he has sent. Rudin # A-35

did.unittitle: Carnegie, Andrew Addressee: Dr. S. Weir Mitchell Writer's location: New York

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Explains reasons for establishing the "Hero Fund". Rudin # A-36

did.unittitle: Carver, George Washington Addressee: Bob Barry Writer's location: Tuskegee Institute, Alabama

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter to peanut company telling them about his research on several peanut diseases. Rudin # A-37

did.unittitle: Carver, George Washington Addressee: Henry H Overton Writer's location: Tuskegee Institute, Alabama

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter sharing the loss of his money during the Depression. Rudin # A-38

did.unittitle: Carver, George Washington Addressee: Grady Porter Writer's location: Tuskegee Institute, Alabama

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter reviewing specimens of fungi. Rudin # A-39

did.unittitle: Carver, George Washington Addressee: "My dear boy, Mr. Davis" Writer's location: Tuskegee Institute, Alabama

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Discusses carefully balanced meals and "putting of the peanut into the home as a regular part of the daily menu". Rudin # A-40

did.unittitle: Carver, George Washington Writer's location: Tuskegee Institute, Alabama

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: a. "The Peanut". b. "How to Grow the Peanut and 105 Ways of Preparing it for Human Consumption". Rudin # A-41

did.unittitle: Catt, Carrie Chapman Addressee: Office of the Secretary Navy Department Writer's location: New York

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter asking for endorsement of woman suffrage. A brochure is attached to the letter. Also included is a broadside warning of women's suffrage. Rudin # A-42

did.unittitle: Churchill, Winston Addressee: Louis A. Sendel Writer's location: London, S.W. 7

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A thank you note for a box of cigars. Rudin # A-43

did.unittitle: Churchill, Winston Addressee: Pamela, Countess of Lytton Writer's location: Chartwell, Westerham, Kent

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Churchill writes of his painting, toiling at the book, memories of better times and his thoughts of the position of independent India. Rudin # A-44

did.unittitle: Clay, Henry Addressee: John Howard Payne Esq. Writer's location: Washington

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Free-franked address panel signed by Clay. Rudin # A-45

did.unittitle: Clay, Henry Writer's location: Fayette County Circuit Court

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A legal deposition signed "Clay". Rudin # A-46

did.unittitle: Cohan, George M. Addressee: George Bucks Writer's location: n.p.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Cohan's humorous response to the letter writer who requested that he write 700-800 words for an "Anniversary Number". Rudin # A-47

did.unittitle: Colt, Samuel Addressee: To the Editor of the New York Tribune Writer's location: Hartford, CT

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Important Civil War letter written to assure the Editor that his company is dedicated to the Federal Government and not to aiding the rebels in the Southern States. Rudin # A-48

did.unittitle: Cosey, Joseph Addressee: Writer's location: Philadelphia

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Cosey forgery of Benjamin Franklin signature. Rudin # M-57

did.unittitle: Cosey, Joseph Addressee: Writer's location: Philadelphia

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Cosey forgery of Abraham Lincoln's signature. Rudin # A-57A

did.unittitle: Cox, Archibald Writer's location: n.p.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter regretting Supreme Court's involvement in the 2000 Presidential election. Rudin # A-49

did.unittitle: Cox, Archibald Addressee: Dean Collins Writer's location: Harvard Law School Cambridge, MA

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Explains what he thinks Haig's role was in Watergate cover-up. Rudin # A-50

did.unittitle: Cox, Archibald Addressee: Mr. S. Liuna ? Writer's location: Brooksville, ME

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Gives his opinion regarding the impeachment of President Bill Clinton. Rudin # A-51

did.unittitle: Cox, Archibald Addressee: Mr. Hoffert Writer's location: Brooksville, ME

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter to history teacher answering several questions about Richard Nixon. Rudin # A-52

did.unittitle: Cox, Archibald Addressee: Kelly Web Writer's location: Harvard Law School

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Response to a question asking if Cox believed President Ford acted wisely in pardoning Nixon. Rudin # A-53

did.unittitle: Cox, Archibald Addressee: Alex Oblado Writer's location: Brooksville, ME

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "Would you not agree that President Nixon's wrongdoing was more dangerous to the country than President Clinton's?" Rudin # A-54

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: As Watergate special prosecutor, Cox states "Even the president must be subject to the law" Rudin # A-54A

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Monopoly Board Game Patent by Charles B. Darrow. Rudin # M-82

did.unittitle: Darrow, Clarence Addressee: Dr. Theodore Perceval Gerson Writer's location: Chicago

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Personal letter about a Los Angeles trial. Rudin # A-55

did.unittitle: Darwin, Charles Addressee: Ferdinand Julius Cohn Writer's location: Down, Beckenham, Kent

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter to a German botanist discussing recently published observations made by Darwin's son. Rudin # A-56

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: See the Gail and Stephen Rudin Civil War Collection, #4696.

did.unittitle: Deforest, Lee Addressee: Thomas K. Boyce Writer's location: Los Angeles, CA

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Responds to a question about today's television. Accompanied by patent sketch of electrical current amplification device (Actually, a first day of issue of the 11 cent "Progress in Electronic" stamp). Rudin # A-61

did.unittitle: Deforest, Lee Addressee: Mr. Olsen Writer's location: Los Angeles, CA

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Father of radio comments on the aid to democracy talking pictures and television can be. Rudin # A-62

did.unittitle: Dix, Dorothea Addressee: His Excellency the Governor Writer's location: Raleigh, SC

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Writes letter thanking the General Assembly of SC for their vote to pay for the construction of a hospital for the insane. Rudin # A-63

did.unittitle: Dix, Dorothea Addressee: Unknown Writer's location: Washington, DC

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Dix pleads with recipient not to send nurses who do not appear to be "matronly". Rudin # A-64

did.unittitle: Dix, Dorothea Writer's location: Washington, DC

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: General Orders 351 - On employment of women nurses during the Civil War. Rudin # A-65

did.unittitle: Earhart, Amelia Addressee: Robert James Braly Writer's location: New York City

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Predicts the future of aviation. Rudin # A-66

did.unittitle: Earhart, Amelia Addressee: Dr. R.E. Whitehead Writer's location: Rye, New York

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Earhart suggests statistics should be gathered for women pilots "We do not know how they react to altitude, or fatigue, or anything else, as we do men." Rudin # A-66A

did.unittitle: Earhart, Amelia Addressee: Miss. Ursula Toomey Writer's location: New York City

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Earhart is pleased that more women are showing an interest in aviation. Rudin # A-67

did.unittitle: Earhart, Amelia Addressee: Federal Communications Commission Writer's location: Union Air Terminal Burbank, California

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Earhart licensing the aircraft for her last flight. Rudin # A-68

did.unittitle: Edison, Thomas Writer's location: Orange, NJ

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Edison's working draft of a letter relating to foreign royalties on the photograph. Rudin # A-69

did.unittitle: Edison, Thomas Writer's location: n.p.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Questions and answers posed to civil engineers and mechanical engineers seeking employment at Edison Laboratories. Rudin # A-70

did.unittitle: Edison, Thomas Addressee: Buchanan Writer's location: n.p.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Asks departments to reduce payrolls. Rudin # A-71

did.unittitle: Edison, Thomas Writer's location: Menlo Park, NJ

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Electric Lamp Patent to provide the lamp with a simpler and cheaper base. See also Selden, Bell, Tesla and Wright for other patents. Rudin # A-72

did.unittitle: Edison, Thomas Addressee: Hon. Abram S. Hewitt Writer's location: New York

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Edison agrees to light Cooper Institute. Rudin # A-73

did.unittitle: Edison, Thomas Addressee: Edwin Elsbach Writer's location: Orange, NJ

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Discusses his experiments on ink which could facilitate printing for the blind. Rudin # A-74

did.unittitle: Edison, Thomas Writer's location: West Orange, NJ

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Minutes of a meeting of the Board of Directors of Thomas A. Edison, Inc. discussing calculations of costs of various models of phonographs as well as the pricing of a main spring lubricant. Rudin # A-75

did.unittitle: Edison, Thomas Writer's location: West Orange, NJ

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Minutes of a Meeting of the Board of Directors of Phonographs, Limited - Edison elected President of the Corporation. Rudin # A-76

did.unittitle: Edison, Thomas Addressee: Buchanan Writer's location: (Orange, NJ)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter informing that the demand for records will fall off on account of...radio. Rudin # A-77

did.unittitle: Edison, Thomas Writer's location: West Orange, NJ

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Minutes of a Special Meeting of the Stockholders of Edison Phonograph Distributing Company to approve the merger with Phonograph Corp. of Indiana. Rudin # A-78

did.unittitle: Edison, Thomas Writer's location: n.p.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Original pencil electrical circuit drawing. Sound equipment for recording music for phonographs. Rudin # A-79

did.unittitle: Edison, Thomas Addressee: W.H. Hand Writer's location: West Orange, NJ

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A misprinted check signed by Thomas Edison. Rudin # A-80

did.unittitle: Edison, Thomas Addressee: Edward Cranch Writer's location: Orange, NJ

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Edison discusses the phonograph and optimistically reports on record production after a fire destroyed his plant. Rudin # A-81

did.unittitle: Einstein, Albert Addressee: Robert FriedmanWriter's location: Princeton, NJ

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Einstein congratulates the addressee for his work with Jewish refugees. He states, "We have no other means of self defense than our solidarity and our knowledge that the cause for which we are suffering is a momentous and sacred cause." Rudin # A-81A

did.unittitle: Ellsworth, Oliver Addressee: John Lawrence, Treasurer Writer's location: Hartford, CT

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Revolutionary War document dates 7/4/1776 requesting payment for boarding officers, prisoners of war. Rudin # A-82

did.unittitle: Ervin, Sam Addressee: Steven Rubin Writer's location: Morganton, NC

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Ervin believes President Ford should not have pardoned Nixon. Rudin # A-83

did.unittitle: King Ferdinand and Queen Isabella Writer's location: Medina del Campo

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: King and Queen of Spain -- signed manuscript document ordering payment to workers who hadn't been paid. Rudin # A-84

did.unittitle: Ford, Henry Addressee: Colonel L.S. Horner Writer's location: Detroit

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Comments on how the Liberty motor (in airplanes) contributed to ending the war. Rudin # A-85

did.unittitle: Franklin, Benjamin Writer's location: Philadelphia

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notarization of petition of bankruptcy. Rudin # A-86

did.unittitle: Franklin, Benjamin Writer's location: Province of Pennsylvania

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Twenty shilling bank note printed by B. Franklin and D. Hall. Rudin # A-86A

did.unittitle: Freud, Anna Addressee: Mr. Baker Writer's location: London

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter from Freud's daughter adhering to his wishes to protect his private life. Rudin # A-87

did.unittitle: Gandhi, Indira Addressee: Mr. Paul Dennett Writer's location: New Delhi

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Her definition as to what politics means. Rudin # A-88

did.unittitle: Gandhi, Indira Addressee: Mr. John Simmons Writer's location: New Delhi

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Philosophically comments on life and history. Rudin # A-89

did.unittitle: Gandhi, Mohandas Writer's location: Bombay Congress Bulletin

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Inflammatory newsletter promoting Indian independence. Rudin # A-90

did.unittitle: Garrison, William Lloyd Addressee: Wm H. Sweetser Writer's location: Boston

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: What ever may be the color of his skin, "a man's a man for a' that". Rudin # A-91

did.unittitle: Garrison, William Lloyd Addressee: Doctor Church Writer's location: Boston

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Garrison helps organize anti-slavery lectures and names leading figures of the day in the abolitionist movement. Rudin # A-92

did.unittitle: Gatling, R.J. Addressee: Mr. Welles Writer's location: Hartford, CT

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Gatling suggests using the improved Gatling Gun to protect the Treasury Building in Washington, D.C. Rudin # A-93

did.unittitle: George III Addressee: Richard Rigby, Paymaster General Writer's location: Court at St. James's

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The King directs payment to General Sir Jeffrey Amherst. Rudin # A-94

did.unittitle: George V Addressee: "My dear Baron" Writer's location: Sandringham, Norfolk

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Thank you note the birth of his son, George IV. Rudin # A-95

did.unittitle: Gershwin, George Addressee: "Dear Walter" Writer's location: n.p.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter discussing his satisfaction with "Tip Toes" and his work on "Oh Kay!" Rudin # A-96

did.unittitle: Getty, Jean Paul Addressee: "Dear parents" Writer's location: Hamburg, Germany

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Lengthy letter to his parents about his travel, his finances and his disgust that his parents don't write to him more "oftener". Rudin # A-97

did.unittitle: Getty, Jean Paul Addressee: To his father Writer's location: Vienna, Austria

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Writes his father about his father's poor business-making decisions. He fails to understand his father's attitude towards him as he approaches his twenty first birthday. Rudin # A-98

did.unittitle: Getty, Jean Paul Addressee: "Dear father and mother" Writer's location: Pall Mall, London

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Getty discusses business and the fact that money is so visible - comments on the many Rolls Royce's he sees. Rudin # A-99

did.unittitle: Getty, Jean Paul Addressee: "Dearest father" Writer's location: Pall Mall, London

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Discusses production of oil wells, financial conditions in London and his contacts. Rudin # A-100

did.unittitle: Getty, Jean Paul Addressee: Gerald M. Loeb Writer's location: Guilford, Surrey

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "I have never considered myself a business man but rather a man who is interested in many things, including business". Rudin # A-101

did.unittitle: Getty, Jean Paul Addressee: Gerald M. Loeb Writer's location: Guilford, Surrey

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Four letter correspondence regarding the book "Battle for Stock Market Profits". Rudin # A-102

did.unittitle: Goldman, Emma Addressee: Mr. Elsbach

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Goldman writes on the subject of voting. Rudin # A-103

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Al Gore's Concession Speech - Presidential Election - 2000. Rudin # A-104

did.unittitle: Greatbatch, Wilson - the inventor of the Pacemaker Writer's location: n.p.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The inventor of the pacemaker draws a diagram of the inner workings of a heart pacemaker. Rudin # A-105

did.unittitle: Greeley, Horace Addressee: A.M. Ide, Jr. Writer's location: New York

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Offers a word of counsel about writing. Rudin # A-106

did.unittitle: Greeley, Horace Addressee: John D. Baldwin Writer's location: New York

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Greeley shows his determination to do what is right regardless of consequences. Rudin # A-107

did.unittitle: Greeley, Horace Addressee: "Dear Sir" Writer's location: Office of the Tribune New York

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: He advises an Englishman to "Go West". Rudin # A-108

did.unittitle: Greeley, Horace Addressee: Mr. O.D. Case Writer's location: Office of the Tribune New York

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Complains of delays in the production of the first volume of his book on the Civil War, "The American Conflict". Rudin # A-109

did.unittitle: Hamilton, Alexander Addressee: The President and Directors of the Bank of the United States Writer's location: Philadelphia

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Hamilton requests a loan of $800,000 to enable the government to operate for the year 1793. Rudin # A-110

did.unittitle: Hammerstein, Oscar Addressee: R.A. Wachsman Writer's location: Doylestown, PA

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Doesn't recollect "Make Believe" ever being considered a river song. Rudin # A-111

did.unittitle: Hearst, William Randolph Addressee: Mr. Norcross

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Hearst gives instructions to an editor for a forthcoming magazine issue Rudin # A-112

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Hancock, as President of the Continental Congress, assigns a Captain to a company in the Army. Rudin # A-112

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Takes responsibility for any financial obligations assumed by his friend, Philip Payne. Rudin # A-113

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Discusses a new play about to open in Berlin. (In German with translation). Rudin # A-114

did.unittitle: Hirschfeld, Al Addressee: Mr. and Mrs. William Post, Jr. Writer's location: New York, NY

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter of thanks to good friends with amusing sketch on envelope and self portrait on letter. Rudin # A-115

did.unittitle: Hirschfeld, Al Addressee: Barry Lindeman Writer's location: New York, NY

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Response to an admirer looking for a freebie. Rudin # A-116

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Original signed ink self-portrait. With First Day of Issue postage stamps designed by Hirschfeld, of comedians and silent screen stars. Rudin # A-117

did.unittitle: Holmes, Oliver Wendell Addressee: Vaughan Writer's location: Boston

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Writes about his upcoming appointment to the U.S. Supreme Court. Rudin # A-118

did.unittitle: Howe, Julia Ward Writer's location: New York London

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: First printing of "The Battle Hymn of the Republic" in The Atlantic Monthly. Rudin # A-119

did.unittitle: Howe, Julia Ward Addressee: Mrs. L. Imman Writer's location: Newport, RI

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: She has read with great pleasure of a Women's Suffrage Society being formed. Rudin # A-120

did.unittitle: Humphrey, Hubert H. Addressee: Chester Kerr, Director of Yale University Press Writer's location: Washington, D.C.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: His thoughts on the management of the Democratic Convention in Chicago. Rudin # A-121

did.unittitle: Kallir, Otto Addressee: Louis Caldor Writer's location: New York

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Archive of art dealings with Grandma Moses and her agent Louis Caldor. See also Grandma Moses. Rudin # A-122

did.unittitle: Kaufman, George S. Addressee: Edna Ferber Writer's location: New York City
did.physdesc: TLS & Article Typed 7" x 8.5" - Ltr 4to - Article

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Six page transcript of an article he was writing about Ferber for Cosmopolitan. Also with a cover letter to Ferber. Rudin # A-123

did.unittitle: Kaufman, George S. Addressee: Edna Ferber Writer's location: Holicong, PA

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Speaks of joint plans for collaboration. Rudin # A-124

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Signed photograph in her graduation robe. Rudin # A-125

did.unittitle: Keller, Helen Addressee: Edwin Elsbach Writer's location: Wrentham, Mass.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Writes her feelings about soldiers who have lost their sight during the war. Rudin # A-126

did.unittitle: Keller, Helen Addressee: Elmer V. GriggsWriter's location: Forest Hills, NY

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Keller writes a heartfelt remembrance on the influence of Anne Sullivan in her life. This letter is also a statement of thanks to a generous contributor to the American Foundation for the Blind. Rudin # A-126A

did.unittitle: Kennedy, Jacqueline Addressee: Mrs. Moody Writer's location: New York

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Writes to Caroline's school about the relationship and effect of the Secret Service on her daughter Caroline. Rudin # A-127

did.unittitle: Kennedy, Jacqueline Addressee: Eleanor Beard Writer's location: Hardinsburg, KY

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Orders slipper tips with accompanying swatches of blue velvet and receipts. Rudin # A-128

did.unittitle: King, Jr. Martin Luther Addressee: Jacob Bacal Writer's location: Atlanta, Georgia

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Thanks a supporter "convinced that we stand on the threshold of our nation's bright tomorrow". Also includes commemorative stamps and envelopes. Rudin # A-130

did.unittitle: Kinsey, Alfred C. Addressee: Peter Briggs Writer's location: Bloomington, Indiana

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Relationship between male sex hormones and cancer. Rudin # A-131

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: See the Gail and Stephen Rudin Simon Lake Collection #6992

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: See the Gail and Stephen Rudin Civil War Collection,#4696, Rudin # A-139

did.unittitle: Lindbergh, Charles Addressee: Ray Fernstrom Writer's location: New York City

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Lindbergh looks forward to a time when transatlantic aviation is a relatively safe commercial enterprise. Rudin # A-140

did.unittitle: Lindbergh, Charles Addressee: "Dear Anne" (His Wife) Writer's location: Washington, DC

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Lindbergh writes his wife after returning from Europe amid negative press for his views on World War II. Takes pains to shield his children from publicity. Rudin # A-141

did.unittitle: Lindbergh, Charles Writer's location: Disuked (Philippines)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Lindberg writes of his wildlife conservation efforts in the Philippines and his regard for the establishment of the Private Association for National Minorities. Rudin # A-142

did.unittitle: Lindbergh, Charles Writer's location: Vaud, Switzerland

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: On book royalties, Freemasonry, and fund raising. Rudin # A-143

did.unittitle: Lindbergh, Charles Writer's location: Detroit, MI

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "Charles Lindbergh" Cover - 22 Kt. Gold Stamp replica. Rudin # A-144

did.unittitle: Lindbergh, Charles Addressee: Mr. Joseph Klingler Writer's location: St. Louis, MO

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Air mail envelope "Lindbergh Again Flies The Air Mail". Rudin # A-145

did.unittitle: Lindbergh, Charles Addressee: Clyde Kelly Writer's location: n.p.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter arguing for extending air mail service to the countries of Central America. Rudin # A-145A

did.unittitle: Lindbergh, Charles Addressee: Mr. L. B. Gatchell Writer's location: Cristobal to Miami

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: First non-stop regular Air Mail flight Cristobal to Miami by Lindbergh. Rudin # A-146

did.unittitle: Lindbergh, Charles Addressee: Bill Schneider Jr. Writer's location: Cienfuegos to Jamaica

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: First flight Cienfuegos to Jamaica by Lindbergh. Rudin # A-147

did.unittitle: Lindbergh, Charles Writer's location: Chicago, Illinois

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: First Solo Transatlantic Flight-1927 / First Day of Issue stamp and cover. Rudin # A-148

did.unittitle: Lindbergh, Charles Addressee: Edward J. Meehan Writer's location: Havana, Cuba

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1828 Cuba Lindbergh Air Mail First Day Cover. Rudin # A-148A

did.unittitle: Lindbergh, Charles Addressee: Mr. Christopher, Chairman of Contest Committee Writer's location: Roosevelt Field, Long Island NY

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A signed time certification for the arrival of Lindbergh and his wife at Roosevelt Field, Long Island, NY - Setting a transcontinental speed record from Los Angeles to Roosevelt Field. Rudin # A-149

did.unittitle: Lindbergh, Charles Addressee: W.R. Ware Writer's location: Chicago - St. Louis

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Postal cover flown Air Mail on the first flight from Chicago to St. Louis. Rudin # A-150

did.unittitle: Lindbergh, Charles Writer's location: Illinois

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photo showing Lindbergh loading air mail for first Illinois CAM flight #2. Rudin # A-151

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Lindbergh flying his plane to Roosevelt Field for takeoff on his transatlantic flight. Rudin # A-152

did.unittitle: Lindbergh, Charles Writer's location: Nashville

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Nashville Banner headline "Lindbergh Takes Off For Paris". Rudin # A-153

did.unittitle: Lindbergh, Charles Writer's location: New York

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: New York Times headline "Lindbergh Does It!". Rudin # A-154

did.unittitle: Lindbergh, Charles Writer's location: New York

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: New York Times headline "Lindbergh's Own Story of Epochal Flight...". Rudin # A-155

did.unittitle: Lindbergh, Charles Writer's location: Albany, NY

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Knickerbocker Press headline "Millions in Lindbergh Spectacle". Rudin # A-155A

did.unittitle: Lindbergh, Charles Writer's location: n.p.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Signed Spirit of St. Louis Poster. Rudin # A-156

did.unittitle: Lindbergh, Charles Addressee: Mr. Everett Cassagneres Writer's location: Darien, CT

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Lindbergh debunks the myth about the title of his book, "We", discusses the design of the "Spirit of St. Louis", and tells an amusing story about a flight over the Grand Canyon. Rudin # A-156A

did.unittitle: Lindbergh, Charles Writer's location: Garden City, NY

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Charles Lindbergh's Solo Transatlantic Flight - 75th Anniversary Folio. Rudin # A-157

did.unittitle: Livermore, Mary Addressee: Mrs. Cole Writer's location: Melrose

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: She is pleased to present a gift on behalf of the American Woman Suffrage Association to Henry Blackwell. Rudin # A-158

did.unittitle: Lodge, Henry Cabot Addressee: E.E. Gaylord, Esq. Writer's location: United States Senate

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Lodge discusses German surrender terms. Rudin # A-159

did.unittitle: Lodge, Henry Cabot Addressee: Charles G. Washburn Writer's location: United States Senate

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Lodge's opinions on Wilson's thoughts and actions in regard to future wars. Rudin # A-160

did.unittitle: MacArthur, Douglas Addressee: To The People Of The Philippines Writer's location: General Headquarters Southwest Pacific Area

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Printed Proclamation - "I have returned". Rudin # A-161

did.unittitle: MacArthur, Douglas Addressee: Charles l. Dean National Commander - National Society - Army of the Philippines Writer's location: General Headquarter Southwest Pacific Area

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Douglas McArthur promises Philippine veterans to raise their flag over Corregidor. (With supporting letter from Commander Dean). Rudin # A-162

did.unittitle: Malcolm X Writer's location: n.p.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Malcolm X proofreads and approves a portion of his 1964 interview conducted by Alex Hailey. Rudin # A-163

did.unittitle: Marconi, Guglielmo Addressee: T.C. Martini Writer's location: South Wellfleet, MA

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Marconi mentions the wireless transmission made by Teddy Roosevelt to King Edward VII. Rudin # A-164

did.unittitle: Marshall, George C. Addressee: Dr. Watson Wharton Writer's location: Pinehurst, NC

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Marshall on military disciplines. Rudin # A-165

did.unittitle: Marshall, John Addressee: William B. Sprague Writer's location: Richmond, VA

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Marshall comments on the evils of alcohol and praises the effort of the early temperance movements in the U.S. Rudin # A-166

did.unittitle: Meir, Golda Addressee: Theodore Cohn Writer's location: n.p.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The solidarity of the Jewish people will overcome its obstacles. Rudin # A-167

did.unittitle: Mitchell, William Lendrum "Billy" Addressee: Robert J. Braly Writer's location: Middleburg, VA

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Writes about the future of air travel. Rudin # A-168

did.unittitle: Morse, Samuel F.B. Addressee: Lt. Col. John R. Leslie Writer's location: New York

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Morse explains the true meaning of an autograph Rudin # A-169

did.unittitle: Morse, Samuel F.B. Addressee: Lt. Col. John R. Leslie Writer's location: New York

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "A little instrument...constructed for...using ...embossed paper for automatic transmission of dispatches". Rudin # A-170

did.unittitle: Grandma Moses Addressee: Louis Caldor Writer's location: Eagle Bridge, NY

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Discusses Caldor's separation from his wife - Moses also mentions her art. Rudin # A-171

did.unittitle: Grandma Moses Addressee: Louis Caldor Writer's location: Eagle Bridge, NY

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Grandma Moses writes about her methods, limitations and her materials. Rudin # A-172

did.unittitle: Grandma Moses Addressee: Louis Caldor Writer's location: Eagle Bridge, NY

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "I will not paint for anyone else...It is strange what some people like...". Rudin # A-173

did.unittitle: Grandma Moses Addressee: Louis Caldor Writer's location: Postmarked Hoosick Falls, NY

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Grandma Moses wants to sell some of her pictures into the Albany Institute of Art and "strike while the iron is hot". Rudin # A-174

did.unittitle: Grandma Moses Addressee: Mrs. Robert Kent ("My Dear Girl Hazle") Writer's location: Eagle Bridge, NY

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Writes of her increasing popularity, her visit to NY and her mortality. Rudin # A-175

did.unittitle: Grandma Moses Addressee: Louis Caldor Writer's location: Eagle Bridge, NY

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "I'm not sending any paintings to the Albany exhibit as there is not anything in it for me." Also says she has to put aside painting so she can care for some children. Rudin # A-176

did.unittitle: Grandma Moses Addressee: Mr. Caldor Writer's location: Eagle Bridge, NY

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Moses writes Caldor about her boys preparing for the war and about her painting. Rudin # A-177

did.unittitle: Grandma Moses Addressee: Louis Caldor Writer's location: Eagle Bridge, NY

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Mentions a photograph that makes her look "as fierce as a goose with one eye". Talks about all her sales and how busy she is. Tells Caldor "you are the one that set the ball a rolling". Says "I'm asking much more for the paintings" because all her materials cost more. Rudin # A-178

did.unittitle: Grandma Moses Addressee: Mrs. Georgie Adams Writer's location: Eagle Bridge, NY

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Grandma Moses offers advice to an aspiring artist, "You can never tell what you can do till you try". Rudin # A-179

did.unittitle: Grandma Moses Writer's location: Harper & Brothers Publishers New York

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Half red leather book entitled, "My Life's History". No. 250 of 250. See Separate Material below. Rudin # A-180

did.unittitle: Nation, Carry A. Addressee: "Brother" Writer's location: Fox Vaudeville Booking Exchange

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: I was arrested here...someone paid the fine $10...wanted to ask you about bringing a suit before the Supreme Court". Rudin # A-181

did.unittitle: Nelson, Lord Horatio Addressee: The Respective Captains, or Commanders of any of His Majesty's Ships... Writer's location: Nelson's ship "The Victory" off Toulon

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Directs the fleet's captains to put themselves under his Command. Rudin # A-182

did.unittitle: Nightingale, Florence Addressee: Elizabeth Gaskell Writer's location: London

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Nightingale seeks help in establishing a "Soldier's Home" at Gibraltar. Rudin # A-183

did.unittitle: Nightingale, Florence Addressee: Hon. Miss. Campbell Writer's location: London

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Nightingale writes of the problems of filing nursing positions. With book "Florence Nightingale" by Cecil Wordham Smith - Constable, London - 1950. Rudin # A-184

did.unittitle: Nightingale, Florence Addressee: Mr. Needham Writer's location: n.p.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Nightingale paves the road for another nursing school. Rudin # A-185

did.unittitle: O'Keefe Georgia Addressee: James Thrall Soby ? Writer's location: Albuquerque, NM

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Writes of a performance involving bulls that she saw and of an aspiring artist, Arthur Dove. Rudin # A-186

did.unittitle: Parrish, Maxfield Addressee: Frank Chouteau Brown Writer's location: Windsor, Vermont

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Beautifully-penned letter sending a page of lettering. Rudin # A-187

did.unittitle: Pershing, John J. Addressee: Colonel W.C. Church Writer's location: Fort Bliss, Texas

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Pershing urges U.S. preparedness just after World War I has broken out. Rudin # A-188

did.unittitle: Pershing, John J. Addressee: Major General William H. Carter Writer's location: In the Field, Mexico

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Content includes training of soldiers with pistols mounted on horses and his thoughts on universal service and the President of Mexico. Rudin # A-189

did.unittitle: Pittman, Isaac Addressee: Robert Paterson, Junior Writer's location: Bath, England

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Pitman states that shorthand is steadily advancing in England, although slowly as he had expected. (With a one page letter signed by his brother Benn Pitman). Rudin # A-190

did.unittitle: Pulitzer, Joseph Addressee: Governor David Hill Writer's location: New York

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Pulitzer requests the participation of the National Guard at the dedication ceremony of the Statue of Liberty. Additionally, a 3 cent stamp preserved on a Cover commemorating the 100th anniversary of Pulitzer's birth is included. See also Bartholdi. Rudin # A-191

did.unittitle: Rabin, Yitzhak Addressee: John F. Cleary III Writer's location: Jerusalem, Israel

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Rabin commenting on his receiving the Nobel Peace Prize. Rudin # A-192

did.unittitle: Rickover, Hiram G. Addressee: The Honorable John E. Moss Joint Committee on Atomic Energy Writer's location: At Sea, North Atlantic

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Returning from sea trials of the nuclear powered aircraft carrier USS Nimitz, Rickover discusses the future of the nuclear carrier task force. Rudin # A-193

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Two page signed copy of Andrew Riker's will leaving his island to his sons. Also included is a four page Abstract of Title giving additional information about the heredity of Riker's Island. Rudin # M-92

did.unittitle: Rockwell, Norman Addressee: Mr. Julian Bach, Jr. Writer's location: New Rochelle, NY

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Rockwell praises the beauty of American women. Rudin # A-194

did.unittitle: Rockwell, Norman Addressee: Miss. Mabel Saloomey Writer's location: n.p.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: He tells a student how to make a picture more realistic. Pictures should tell stories. Rudin # A-195

did.unittitle: Rockwell, Norman Addressee: Wm. Harold Martin Writer's location: Arlington, VT

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A letter responding to an idea for a painting that was sent to him. Rudin # A-196

did.unittitle: Rockwell, Norman Addressee: Mrs. Frank R. Dorlay Writer's location: Stockbridge, MA

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter of thanks for a model offer. Rudin # A-197

did.unittitle: Rockwell, Norman Addressee: Mr. Hudson Writer's location: Stockbridge, MA

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: States that he paints pictures of many important people from photographs taken by his photographer. Rudin # A-198

did.unittitle: Rockwell, Norman Addressee: Mr. Brian Mitton Writer's location: Stockbridge, MA

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter recommending attendance schools in order to learn to draw. Includes an original sketch of a dog. Rudin # A-199

did.unittitle: Rodgers, Richard Addressee: Miss. Jean Dalrymple Writer's location: New York, NY

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Rodgers' personal statement of his own feelings on music. Rudin # A-200

did.unittitle: Rogers, Will Addressee: Edgar W. Cooley Writer's location: n.p.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Defends charges that he is un-American. (With associated correspondence). Rudin # A-201

did.unittitle: Rogers, Will Writer's location: Hot Springs, FL

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Full length silhouette signed. Includes Rogers stamps and envelopes. Rudin # A-202

did.unittitle: Roosevelt, Eleanor Addressee: Mr. Clayton Wheeler Writer's location: Eastport, ME

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "Franklin…had a mild case of poliomyelitis…but the doctors hope for a complete recovery." Also included in this file is an archive of Franklin Roosevelt - Clayton Wheeler letters, as well as Eleanor Roosevelt photos and stamps, and a Franklin Roosevelt Memorial Coin. Rudin # A-203

did.unittitle: Roosevelt, Eleanor Addressee: Mr. Sladdin Writer's location: New York, NY

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "Paintings of myself are not my idea of joy!" Rudin # A-204

did.unittitle: Roosevelt, Eleanor Addressee: Mr. Lewis C. Reimann Writer's location: New York

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "I am dry personally...temperance must be preached..."; included is a copy of her 12/9/1932 radio speech about the girl of today and a copy of an "Excerpt from letter from Methodist Minister of Kansas" regarding drinking. Rudin # A-204A

did.unittitle: Roosevelt, Eleanor Addressee: Mr. Crowinshield Editor, Vanity Fair Writer's location: The White House Washington

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Declining an appointment to have a photograph taken. "I am not particularly fond of photographs". Rudin # A-205

did.unittitle: Roosevelt, Eleanor Addressee: "Tiny" (Mayris Chaney) Writer's location: Washington DC

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "It was a great shock but I'm so glad there was no pain, no long illness". Rudin # A-206

did.unittitle: Roosevelt, Eleanor Addressee: "Uncle Fred" (Frederick Adrian Delano) Writer's location: New York City

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Eleanor indicates that FDR wanted a memorial similar to Thomas Jefferson. Rudin # A-207

did.unittitle: Roosevelt, Eleanor Addressee: Mr. Heuschkel Writer's location: New York, NY

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "It is never enough to simply vote." Rudin # A-208

did.unittitle: Roosevelt, Eleanor Addressee: Mr. Salgo Writer's location: New York, NY

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: FDR never allowed anyone to sign his name after he became President. With printed free-franked envelope. Rudin # A-209

did.unittitle: Roosevelt, Eleanor Addressee: Mr. Moore Writer's location: New York, NY

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "History should not be written from casual remarks...there is no necessity to lie ever...". Rudin # A-210

did.unittitle: Roosevelt, Eleanor Addressee: (Her children) Anna, James, Elliott, Franklin Jr. and John Writer's location: n.p.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Eleanor writes to her children regarding the production of a play or motion picture based on the life of President Franklin D. Roosevelt. Rudin # A-210A

did.unittitle: Roosevelt, Theodore Jr. Addressee: Rabbi Stephen S. WiseWriter's location: Washington, D.C.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "The right approach is "What can I do for the country. What can I put into the United States". Forty years before JFK said somewhat the same thing. As part of a letter supporting Joseph Levine as Bronx Borough President. Rudin # A-210B

did.unittitle: Rusk, Dean Addressee: Jim Bishop Writer's location: Athens, GA

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: His comments on the assassination of JFK. Rudin # A-211

did.unittitle: Rusk, Dean Addressee: Mr. John Bullard Writer's location: Athens. GA

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Rusk states that it is untrue that civilian control tied the hands of the military, which prolonged the war. Rudin # A-212

did.unittitle: Russell, Bertrand Addressee: Mrs. Mitchison Writer's location: London S.W.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Discusses his theories on raising children and on education. Rudin # A-213

did.unittitle: Russell, Bertrand Addressee: Ruth Frank Rosenwald Writer's location: Merioneth

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Russell expresses his opposition to nuclear war. Rudin # A-214

did.unittitle: Sadat, Anwar Addressee: Milton J. Shapp Governor of Pennsylvania Writer's location: Cairo, Egypt

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter expressing efforts to bring peace and "to destroy the psychological barriers obstructing the way towards stability and peace". With cover letter from Ashraf Ghorbal, Ambassador. Rudin # A-215

did.unittitle: Sanger, Margaret Addressee: W.H. Perry Writer's location: New York, NY

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Asking for support to make a Middle Western Birth Control Conference a success. Rudin # A-216

did.unittitle: Sanger, Margaret Addressee: Mr. Donahue Writer's location: New York, NY

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter to a supporter summarizing the importance of making birth control available in all communities. Rudin # A-217

did.unittitle: Schweitzer, Albert Addressee: Robert S. McNamara Norman Cousins Writer's location: Lambarene Lambarene

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Two letters that express his views on atomic war. Rudin # A-218

did.unittitle: Selden, George B. Writer's location: Rochester, NY

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: First United States Patent for gasoline driven car. See also Bell, Edison, Tesla and Wright for other patents. Rudin # A-219

did.unittitle: Shackleton, Ernest H. Addressee: Kennar, R.N. Writer's location: London S.W.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Replies to a letter from a fellow explorer and wishes for a successful expedition to discover the South Pole. Rudin # A-220

did.unittitle: Sheen, Bishop Fulton Addressee: William E. Dugan Writer's location: n.p.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "Very often the kind acts of men in the commercial world go unrewarded; too often their good deeds are accepted as duties". See also JFK letter to Arthur Krock in collection #6844. Rudin # A-221

did.unittitle: Sherman, William T. Addressee: General Henry W. Halleck Writer's location: Head-Quarters Military Division of the Mississippi Kingston, GA

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Telegram giving intelligence of his movements during the first weeks of his march through Georgia. Rudin # A-222

did.unittitle: Sikorsky, Igor Writer's location: Bridgeport, CT

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A letter of consideration to the Collier Trophy Committee for the S-42 trans-oceanic flying boat to merit the highest award in the aircraft field. Included is a 2 page memorandum from I.I.Sikorsky. Rudin # A-223

did.unittitle: Smith, Samuel Francis Writer's location: n.p.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The entire four stanzas of our National Hymn "America". (Plus a supposed fifth stanza). Rudin # A-224

did.unittitle: Stanton, Elizabeth Cady Addressee: Mrs. Underwood Writer's location: n.p. (Europe)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Stanton's expresses her feelings on the extremes of wealth and poverty as well as her thoughts on the roles of women. Rudin # A-225

did.unittitle: Stevens, John Paul Writer's location: n.p.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Dissenting opinion in the case of Bush v. Gore, signed by Justice John Paul Stevens. Rudin # A-227

did.unittitle: Stevenson, Adlai Addressee: Sister Mary Linus Writer's location: New York

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The practical reasons for studying history. Rudin # A-228

did.unittitle: Stone, Lucy Addressee: Mrs. Clark Writer's location: Dorchester

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Wants churches to support suffrage of women. Rudin # A-229

did.unittitle: Sullivan, Anne Addressee: Thomas Henderson Writer's location: South Arcan, Muir of Ord, Ross-shire

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Sullivan reflects on the significance of her work, both for herself and for the larger cause of the handicapped. Accepts a fellowship from the teachers of Scotland. Rudin # A-230

did.unittitle: Teller, Edward Addressee: Mr. B.H. McGee Writer's location: Stanford, California

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Technology, the application of man's knowledge, is his best defense against arbitrary power. Rudin # A-231

did.unittitle: Tesla, Nikola Writer's location: United States Patent Office

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "Apparatus for Utilizing Effects Transmitted Through Natural Media" Patent. See also Bell, Edison, Selden and Wright for other patents. Rudin # A-232

did.unittitle: Tombaugh, Clyde, W. Addressee: Jerry Granat Writer's location: Las Cruces, New Mexico

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Answers questions posed to him about the universe by Jerry Granat. Rudin # A-233

did.unittitle: Tombaugh, Clyde, W. Addressee: Mr. Fisher Writer's location: Mesilla Park, NM

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "I do not think there is a 10th planet". Rudin # A-234

did.unittitle: Truman, Margaret Writer's location: Beaumont, TX

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Signed cover of music recital program. (With a second unsigned program). Rudin # A-235

did.unittitle: Trumbull, Jonathan Addressee: Ezekiel Williams Commissary of Prisoners Writer's location: Lebanon, Conn.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: (see also William Wall letter dated 7/8/1780) Permit for a Captain to pass to Providence in exchange for Major Wall during the Revolution. Rudin # A-236

did.unittitle: Victoria, Queen of Great Britain Addressee: Sovereign Land grave of Hesse Homburg (Ferdinand) Writer's location: Windsor Castle

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Birth announcement of George V. Rudin # A-237

did.unittitle: Wall, William Addressee: Major Samuel White Writer's location: Providence, Rhode Island

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: (see also Jonathon Trumbull letter dated 12/15/1777) About partnering in a vessel. Rudin # A-238

did.unittitle: Warhol, Andy Writer's location: n.p.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Warhol's philosophy on subways and on ghetto space. Rudin # A-239

did.unittitle: Washington, Booker T. Writer's location: Tuskegee Institute Tuskegee, Alabama

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notice that card-playing is against the policy of Tuskegee Institute. Rudin # A-240

did.unittitle: Washington, Booker T. Addressee: John Uri Lloyd Writer's location: Tuskegee Institute Tuskegee, Alabama

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Capitalization of the "N" in the word Negro. Rudin # A-241

did.unittitle: Washington, Booker T. Addressee: Franklin W. Hooper Mrs. A.J. Kaine Writer's location: Tuskegee Institute Tuskegee, Alabama

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Considers speaking at Brooklyn Institute of the Arts (Hooper Letter); Mentions his controversial visit to the White House (Kaine Letter). Originally in the Forbes Collection. Rudin # A-242

did.unittitle: Washington, Booker T. Addressee: Rev. Charles D. Kreider Writer's location: Tuskegee Institute Tuskegee, Alabama

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The girls that attend his school are not really willing to relocate north as useful opportunity is available to them in the south. Rudin # A-243

did.unittitle: Washington, Booker T. Addressee: George Bicknell Writer's location: Tuskegee Institute Tuskegee, Alabama

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Entrance qualifications for students interested in applying to Tuskegee Institute. Rudin # A-244

did.unittitle: Washington, Booker T. Addressee: Frederic S. Hartzell Writer's location: Tuskegee Institute Tuskegee, Alabama

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Washington pays tribute to McKinley and his interest in the Negro people. Rudin # A-245

did.unittitle: Washington, Booker T. Addressee: Caroline Arabella Rankin Writer's location: Tuskegee Institute Tuskegee, Alabama

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Washington asks for Rankin's frank impressions of the Institute. Rudin # A-246

did.unittitle: Watson, John B. Addressee: Henry Goddard Leach Editor, The Forum Writer's location: New York

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "The idea of punishment is psychologically wrong." Rudin # A-246A

did.unittitle: Watt, James Addressee: Davies Giddy Writer's location: Heathfield, Birmingham

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Giving the recipe for making cloth waterproof. Rudin # A-247

did.unittitle: Weizmann, Chiam Addressee: Prof. Chaim Weizmann Mrs. Anita Engle-Berkoff Writer's location: Balleymoney, Co. Antrim London W.C. 1

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Weizmann discusses "the steps which ought to be taken to mobilize all Zionists...for the advancement of our aims in Palestine." With a strong letter to Weizmann of suggestion and support from S. McCoubrey. Rudin # A-248

did.unittitle: Willard, Frances E. Writer's location: n.p.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "Every woman that lives is bound to find out what the best thing she can do, is!" ("Leaf out of a lecture"). Rudin # A-249

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A collection of correspondence, documents, telegrams, and newspaper clippings, all relating to his 1960 interview of Richard Nixon and to the "Confucius Say" phenomenon of the 1940's. Rudin # A-250

did.unittitle: Duke of Windsor - Edward the VIII Addressee: Philip Guedalla, Esq. Writer's location: Government House Nassau

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Talks about BBC broadcasts, new issues of Bahamas stamps, has given up "all hope that those in power today will...ever assign me to a more important mission", and much more. Rudin # A-251

did.unittitle: Wright, Frank Lloyd Addressee: For Your questionnaire To the Record Writer's location: Taliesin West

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "It is my belief that the great Art of Architecture cannot be taught." Rudin # A-252

did.unittitle: Wright, Frank Lloyd Addressee: Mrs. Walter O. Forsman Writer's location: Taliesin West

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Replies to Mrs. Forsman about how he considers the display of pictures important and that he provides for them in his architectural designs. (With letter from Mrs. Forsman to Wright - Letter date 1/25/57). Rudin # A-253

did.unittitle: Wright, Orville Addressee: George F. Kuns Writer's location: Dayton, Ohio

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: States that none of the early machines (except the one for 1903) are still in existence. Mentions his glider flight of 1911. Includes photo. Rudin # A-254

did.unittitle: Wright, Orville Addressee: Wilbur and Orville Wright Writer's location: United States Patent Office

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Flying Machine Patent - 10/14/1913. See also Bell, Edison, Selden and Tesla for other patents. Rudin # A-255

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Color postcard of Wilbur and Orville Wright Memorial Beacon. Rudin # A-254
